matches.rationale: Same Edit tool and the trigger targets edits under repository subdirectories where path README files must be read.
Same Write tool and the trigger targets creation under repository subdirectories where path README files must be read.
Same Edit tool and the rule targets Next.js source and test edits with the required watch-process precondition.
Same Bash tool and the checkout/switch trigger exactly represents switching branches and requiring the bootstrap build.
Same Bash tool and the trigger specifically catches use of the nonexistent flag on next build or dev.
Same Write tool and the trigger targets manual creation of test-suite files that must instead be generated with pnpm new-test.
Same Edit tool and the rule targets introducing setTimeout in test code, the prohibited waiting mechanism.
Same Edit tool and the rule targets introducing deprecated check() calls in tests.
Same Edit tool and the rule targets inline files objects in test fixture configuration.
Same Bash tool and gh pr create is an action that writes a PR description requiring the specified footer.

misses.note: The compiler extracted the watch precondition only for Edit, not Write, so no same-tool rule covers creation of source or integration-test files.
The keep-running instruction is embedded as a comment inside the watch-build code block and no Bash rule captures the ongoing process requirement.

The mode/bundler mapping is expressed through a heading and four-command code block; the compiler reported 'Build Commands / Testing / Linting command reference sections — plain documentation of available commands, no action to bind'.
The explicit end-of-session cleanup requirement has no extracted Bash rule.
The explicit types-versus-build choice has no extracted Bash rule; the compiler broadly reported command reference sections as documentation with 'no action to bind'.
The conditional preference for the filtered build has no extracted Bash rule.
The extracted branch-switch rule covers only one part of this broader trigger and is already the closest match to g11; no rule covers CI push or multi-package changes.
The webpack selection requirement is conveyed by a short lead-in plus commands in a code block, and no extracted Bash rule targets --webpack.
The compiler extracted a Write guard for manually creating test files, but no same-tool Bash rule covers invoking the mandatory generator.
The noninteractive invocation format is primarily documented through comments and a command in a code block; no Bash rule captures it.
The compiler deliberately reported 'Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters; capture output once — a cross-call behavioral pattern, not a single tool-call trigger'.
The compiler deliberately reported 'Run the pr-status script when the user asks about CI failures/PR status — triggered by user intent, not a specific tool call'.

The compiler deliberately grouped this under 'General PR-status triage rules (prioritize blocking failures, assume failures real, reproduce with CI env vars, mode-specific test command) — broad judgment guidance'.

The compiler deliberately grouped this under 'General PR-status triage rules (prioritize blocking failures, assume failures real, reproduce with CI env vars, mode-specific test command) — broad judgment guidance'.
The compiler deliberately grouped same-mode/environment reproduction under general PR-status guidance and called it broad judgment guidance.
The compiler deliberately grouped the mode-specific test requirement under general PR-status guidance and called it broad judgment guidance.
The actionable workflow requires following the referenced skill; the compiler reported 'Specialized Skills list ($pr-status-triage, $flags, etc.) — routing reference, not a rule'.
This appears in the nested CI Analysis Tips list; the compiler's broad PR-triage omission leaves no matching Bash rule.

The command sequence is nested under CI Analysis Tips; the compiler reported 'CI Analysis Tips conditional fix suggestions per failure type (cargo fmt, prettier --write) — reactive to CI output, not a proactive tool-call trigger'.

The command is nested under CI Analysis Tips; the compiler reported 'CI Analysis Tips conditional fix suggestions per failure type (cargo fmt, prettier --write) — reactive to CI output, not a proactive tool-call trigger'.
The action is a nested CI Analysis Tips bullet and no extracted Bash rule targets local reproduction of the failing path.
The mode choice is represented by a heading and code block; the compiler grouped mode-specific test commands into broad judgment guidance.
This is a terse development tip rather than a strongly framed requirement, and no Bash rule captures DEBUG=next:*.
This is a terse development tip rather than a strongly framed requirement, and no Bash rule captures the telemetry environment setting.
The compiler deliberately reported 'NODE_ENV vs __NEXT_DEV_SERVER semantic explanation — informational, not a proscribed action'.
The compiler deliberately reported 'NODE_ENV vs __NEXT_DEV_SERVER semantic explanation — informational, not a proscribed action'.
The sensitivity rule has no extracted Bash trigger; the compiler treated the related secret-handling guidance as too broad to bind safely.
The compiler deliberately reported 'Never print or paste secret values in chat responses/commits/logs — too broad to bind to one tool call without false positives'.
The compiler deliberately reported 'Mirror CI env names/modes but don't inline literal secret values — no regex-detectable trigger'.
The compiler deliberately reported 'If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user — conditional on runtime state, not a tool-call trigger'.

unmatched_extracted.note: This is a real explicit rule forbidding commits of local secret files, but gold does not list that file-specific prohibition.
This is a real explicit SSH-failure rule against changing remotes to HTTPS or other auth workarounds.
This is a real action-bindable preference before force pushes or stack rebases that may hydrate objects.
This is a real explicit prohibition on AI-generated or co-author footers in commits.
This is a real action-bindable commit-message style rule.
This is a real explicit requirement to pre-validate changed files before committing.
This is a real action-bindable requirement for PR description content beyond the footer covered by g32.
This is a real explicit prohibition on marking PRs ready for review.
This is a real explicit precondition for integration tests after source changes, distinct from choosing the focused test mode.
This is a real multi-file feature-flag wiring requirement when editing new flags.
This is a real edit rule requiring Node-only feature flags to be false in edge builds.
This is a real import-boundary rule for react-server-dom-webpack modules.
This is a real restriction on relative internal requires in the app-page build template.
This is a real explicit test-edit prohibition against browser.back() in the described router-act scenario.
This is a real Rust import-style rule with a stated inline-module exception.
This is a real documentation-edit rule requiring accurate highlight line counts.
This is a real review-time rule to flag new nonstandard request-header reads for security review.
